rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DOUGLAS P. AMOS GST EXEMPT TRUST, DATED MARCH 24, 2010

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOANNE ANDERSON REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 25, 2020 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Finding Of Nunc Pro Tunc As To The "Order On Petition Confirming Successor Trustee And Trust Assets (Probate Code Section 850(A)(3)" Issued By This Court On April 6, 2021; For The Reissuance And Confirmation Of That Order As Appropriate; And, For Attorney?S Fees And Costs In An Amount To Be Determined By Proof
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. All parties are ordered to appear on July 29, 2021 at 2:00 p.m. in Department 204 of this Court. Remote appearances are authorized and encouraged. See instructions below. The Court's current tentative ruling on the merits is: Movant Ida Sadler's Motion for a Finding of Nunc Pro Tunc as to the "Order on Petition Confirming Successor Trustee and Trust Assets (Probate Code Section 850(a)(3)" Issued by This Court on April 6, 2021; For the Reissuance and Confirmation of that Order as Appropriate; and for Attorney's Fees and Costs in Amount to Be Determined by Proof is DENIED, with a reservation on the issue of attorney fees. The situation as presented in the Motion is not an instance where a court can issue an order nunc pro tunc. A nunc pro tunc order is intended to give a party the judgment or order it was entitled to, but did not timely receive. "[The Court] cannot, however, change an order which has become final even though made in error, if in fact the order made was that intended to be made." (In re Eckstrom's Est. (1960) 54 Cal. 2d 540, 544.) The Movant's request, if granted, would change the substance of the order regarding who is authorized to act as successor trustee of the Anderson Trust and reaffirm an order that the Court has been told (by this motion) was issued on the basis of a fraudulent and improper petition. If the Movant's allegations are true, the April 6, 2021 Order was obtained by a person who lacked standing to petition for it (i.e. it was filed by neither a trustee of the trust nor an "interested person", Prob. C. section 850(a)(3)). The presentation of a petition by an authorized person is a jurisdictional issue. The Order is void on its face if obtained by an unauthorized person (see Nagel v. P & M Distributors, Inc. (1969) 273 Cal. App. 2d 176, 179-80). The Court is concerned that a fraud on the Court may have occurred in connection with the April 6, 2021 Order, and is considering alerting the State Bar regarding this alleged fraud on the court. Remote appearance is available, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
